formatted_address: 21106 Kenswick Meadows Ct, Humble, TX 77338, USA
pitch_rationale: The homes at this Humble, TX address display classic southern-suburban hip roofs. Shadow lengths cast by eaves onto the ground are moderate relative to the 50-ft scale bar — neither long enough to indicate steep (≥8:12) nor negligible enough to suggest shallow (≤4:12) pitch, aligning best with 6:12. The ridge lines on the hip roofs sit visibly inside but not dramatically compressed within the eave perimeter, consistent with a 6:12 rise-to-run ratio. Shingle texture is moderately discernible from nadir, further corroborating a mid-range pitch. Regional context (Houston-area suburban construction, warm humid climate, hip-roof predominance) strongly supports the 5:12–7:12 band, with 6:12 being the most common standard in this market.
footprint_rationale: Target house identified as the multi-section hip-roof home in the lower-left quadrant with direct driveway access from the Kenswick Meadows Ct cul-de-sac (visible at ~px 170–260, 300–520). Two-section L-shaped footprint traced: main body ~232–408 x, 558–722 y (176×164 px = 37.3×34.8 ft) and a rear southeast extension ~302–435 x, 722–872 y (133×150 px = 28.2×31.8 ft). Combined L-shape area: 48,814 sq px × 0.044964 = 2,195 sqft. Roof is predominantly multi-hip with two main ridges (main body ~24 ft, extension ~17 ft, small rear ~10 ft), eight hip lines at all section corners (~136 ft total), two interior valleys where sections meet (~32 ft), minimal gable/rake element on rear addition (~11 ft), and eaves running around the full perimeter bottom edges (~155 ft, adjusted for interior shared walls and hip geometry). Confidence is moderate-low due to difficulty distinguishing exact property boundaries and roof edge types from satellite obliquity.
